_comment: SOURCE OF TRUTH for MCP <-> agent-skill parity. The ProductClank agent REST API (/api/v1/agents/*) is canonical; the agent skill and this MCP server are BOTH wrappers over it. Every endpoint the skill documents MUST appear here with an `mcp` status. `scripts/check-skill-parity.mjs` fails CI when the skill references an endpoint that is missing here. Paths are normalized: no origin, no /api/v1 prefix, path params written as {id}.

statuses.live: Exposed as an MCP tool today.
statuses.planned: Intended as an MCP tool; not built yet (see `tier`).

endpoints.note: Two tools over one endpoint: suggest_content_campaign (dry_run:true, free preview) + create_content_campaign (dry_run:false, launch, 1000cr). Content = Growth Boost / take_action; submissions + judging stay in the webapp (v1).
MCP reads UserCredits directly via service role (the agent balance endpoint reports the trusted agent's own balance, not the caller's).
Should be user-scoped (exclude posts the caller already claimed) — the agent feed is campaign-scoped today.
BACKEND CHANGE REQUIRED: when caller_user_id is set, author-match the tweet against the CALLER's UserSocial.twitter, not the trusted agent's handle.
Tier 3. $PRO pays the AGENT's wallet and is gated on erc8004_agent_id + allowlist; there is no path to pay a human user's wallet yet.
OAuth connect auto-resolves the user; the connector never registers agents.
Owner-linking is replaced by the OAuth consent flow.
Identity comes from the OAuth session, not a tool.
Key management is not a connector concern.
ERC-8004 pre-registration helper; not relevant to the connector.
Public listing helper; not needed in-connector.
Called server-side by the MCP OAuth /callback (trusted key) to grant per-user billing — not a user-facing tool.
Authorization revocation is handled via Claude connector settings + webapp.
POLICY: keep crypto/USDC top-up OUT of the connector (Anthropic directory bars money movement). Top-up stays on the webapp; the connector only spends a prefunded balance.
Telegram-specific; not relevant to the Claude connector.
Telegram-specific; not relevant to the Claude connector.
